implications: Applicant CV: Lists MSc in Clinical Research, PGCert in Healthcare Research Skills, and roles as Clinical Research Fellow and Project Manager, demonstrating relevant training and experience.

Applicant Research Background: Details formal training (PGCert, MRes, NIHR PI essentials) and experience leading QI projects, trials, and national studies, demonstrating strong methodological alignment.
Training & Development and Research Support: Lists extensive formal training in systematic reviews, qualitative methods, and data analysis software, directly relevant to the proposed study.
Applicant CV: Includes a peer-reviewed publication in The European Respiratory Journal and awards (NIHR Incubator, Brian Turley Award), indicating impactful research outputs.

Applicant Research Background: Lists multiple peer-reviewed publications (BMJ Thorax, Physiotherapy Journal), conference presentations, and contributions to NICE/BTS guidance, evidencing impactful outputs.
Applicant CV: Shows progression from Physiotherapy Technician to Clinical Research Fellow and Lead Emergency Practitioner, with evidence of project management and data coordination responsibilities.
Applicant Research Background: Demonstrates increasing leadership through roles as PI for national studies, Associate PI, and supervising research nurses, alongside clinical team leadership.

Training & Development and Research Support: Includes leadership programmes (King's Fund), research visits to policy bodies, and mentorship development, supporting trajectory towards research leadership.
Applicant CV: Demonstrates a practitioner-academic trajectory with clinical roles (Emergency Practitioner, Physiotherapist) alongside research qualifications and fellowships.

Applicant Research Background: Explicitly states commitment to a practitioner-academic career, balancing senior clinical leadership with research fellow roles and aiming for post-doctoral advancement.
Training & Development and Research Support: Explicitly plans 20% clinical practice to map skills against consultant-level roles and maintain integration within the clinical team.
Applicant Research Background: Clearly articulates a career trajectory from clinical physiotherapist to post-doctoral clinical academic in UEC, supported by a pre-doctoral bridging fellowship.
Training & Development and Research Support: Outlines a bespoke programme aligned with RDF to prepare the applicant as an expert clinical academic, supported by institutional strategic vision.

Applicant Research Background: Explains the DCAF as the necessary next step to build on a pre-doctoral fellowship and MRes, enabling full-time research development previously limited by clinical duties.
Training & Development and Research Support: Details specific training to address identified gaps (e.g., writing, qualitative analysis) and build skills for post-doctoral progression.

Applicant Research Background: Provides strong evidence of prior research quality through successful grant awards (pre-doctoral bridging), PI roles in national studies, and a track record of publications.
Plain English Summary of Research: Clearly outlines problem, objectives, methods, and impact in accessible language, reviewed by PPIE team, supporting pr.1.

Plain English Summary of Research: Describes a four-part study (literature review, interviews, discussions, workshop) with clear tasks, supporting feasibility and scope (pr.3).
Detailed Research Plan: Outlines a feasible 4-work package study with clear aims, sample sizes (30 interviews, 16 focus group participants), and timeline milestones, supporting pr.3.
Plain English Summary of Research: Explicitly lists methods including literature review, interviews, group discussions, and a co-production workshop, supporting pr.4.
Scientific Abstract: Details four work packages including systematic review, interviews, focus groups, and a co-design workshop using realist evaluation, supporting pr.4.

Detailed Research Plan: Provides explicit methodological rigor with systematic review protocols (PRISMA, CASP), qualitative analysis (Thematic, Framework), and sampling strategies (purposive, snowball), supporting pr.4.
Plain English Summary of Research: Highlights patient benefits, service improvement, and a toolkit to standardize care, directly addressing patient and public impact (pr.5).
Scientific Abstract: Links the study to urgent NHS workforce crises and aims to produce a toolkit for sustainable workforce planning, supporting pr.5.
Detailed Research Plan: Clearly articulates impact on patients, services, and policy through a co-produced toolkit to reduce variation and improve workforce planning, supporting pr.5.
Plain English Summary of Research: Details PPIE involvement in study design, material development, and findings dissemination via a PAG, strongly supporting pr.6.
Scientific Abstract: Explicitly mentions co-producing a toolkit via a stakeholder workshop and PAG collaboration, supporting pr.6.
Detailed Research Plan: Demonstrates strong engagement via PAG involvement in design, recruitment of service-users for focus groups, and co-production of the toolkit, supporting pr.6.
Plain English Summary of Research: Describes a Patient and Public Advisory Group (PAG) involved in developing materials and questions, supporting integration of lived experience (wp.1).
Scientific Abstract: Describes co-design of an implementation toolkit with stakeholders, reinforcing integration of lived experience (wp.1).
Detailed Research Plan: Integrates lived experience through PAG involvement in question development and co-design of the toolkit, supporting wp.1.
Patient & Public Involvement: Details co-design activities where patient engagement refined aims, objectives, and recruitment materials, strongly supporting integration of lived experience.
Training & Development and Research Support: Includes specific training in PPIE and co-production to ensure public involvement is embedded throughout the research cycle.
Plain English Summary of Research: Outlines plans to share findings with policymakers, professional bodies, and via journals/conferences to drive next steps, supporting wp.3.
Scientific Abstract: Outlines dissemination via PAG, conferences, and publications to increase service-user awareness, supporting wp.3.
Detailed Research Plan: Outlines comprehensive dissemination plans including conferences, publications, and infographics for service-users to maximize public benefit, supporting wp.3.
Patient & Public Involvement: Explicitly assigns PAG members a leading role in disseminating findings and driving knowledge mobilisation, supporting commitment to public benefit.
Scientific Abstract: Identifies a clear knowledge gap regarding reasons for lack of CP role implementation and unexplored impacts, supporting pr.2.c.
Detailed Research Plan: Critically reviews existing literature, identifying specific gaps in evidence regarding CP role implementation, impact, and evaluation in UEC settings, supporting pr.2.
Detailed Research Plan: Details inclusive recruitment strategies (nationally representative, hybrid options, translation services) and diverse stakeholder levels (micro/meso/macro), supporting pr.9.

Training & Development and Research Support: Identifies specific skill gaps (e.g., reflexivity, NVivo, scientific writing) and maps targeted training to address them.

Detailed Research Plan: Explicitly links the fellowship to a post-doctoral application and career trajectory, demonstrating support for long-term aspirations, supporting td.3.
Training & Development and Research Support: Links training to long-term career goals of becoming an expert clinical academic and securing post-doctoral fellowships.
SUMMARY BUDGET: Includes funding for leadership courses (King's Fund) and knowledge mobilisation training, linking development activities to long-term career aspirations (td.3).
Detailed Research Plan: Describes active supervisor support in review protocols, PAG oversight, and regular progress tracking against milestones, supporting ss.2.
Training & Development and Research Support: Describes a robust supervisory team with specific expertise in health systems, emergency care, and advanced practice, including monthly joint meetings.
Patient & Public Involvement: Describes a socially and ethnically diverse group reflecting the local population and plans for UK-wide representation, supporting inclusion of seldom heard groups.
Patient & Public Involvement: Outlines involvement across the full research lifecycle from proposal development to data analysis and dissemination, supporting inclusivity across stages.
Patient & Public Involvement: Confirms funding for participation is included in the budget and outlines educational support for PAG members, supporting a costed inclusion plan.
SUMMARY BUDGET: Includes specific budget lines for PAG meetings, vouchers, training, and interpretation services, confirming a fully costed plan for research inclusion (wp.6).
Training & Development and Research Support: Highlights the host's strong track record with £15.6m funding, 4* research outputs, and successful NIHR fellowships.
Training & Development and Research Support: Details institutional support including protected time, research forums, TACRI access, and a pathway for future clinical academic roles.
Training & Development and Research Support: Confirms commitment to EDI through Athena Swan Silver Award, Stonewall Diversity Champion status, and adherence to VITAE Concordat.
